title: thymus serpyllum subsp. polytrichus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. polytrichus, also known as wild thyme or creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It possesses medicinal properties due to its high content of essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol, which have antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote better sleep. Wild thyme can be prepared through herbal teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
